description: ProxmoxCluster is the Schema for the proxmoxclusters API.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: ProxmoxClusterSpec defines the desired state of a ProxmoxCluster.

allowedNodes.description:
AllowedNodes specifies all Proxmox nodes which will be considered
for operations. This implies that VMs can be cloned on different nodes from
the node which holds the VM template.

cloneSpec.description:
NodeCloneSpec is the configuration pertaining to all items configurable
in the configuration and cloning of a proxmox VM. Multiple types of nodes can be specified.

additionalProperties.description: ProxmoxMachineSpec defines the desired state of a ProxmoxMachine.

allowedNodes.description:
AllowedNodes specifies all Proxmox nodes which will be considered
for operations. This implies that VMs can be cloned on different nodes from
the node which holds the VM template.

This field is optional and should only be set if you want to restrict
the nodes where the VM can be cloned.
If not set, the ProxmoxCluster will be used to determine the nodes.

checks.description: Checks defines possibles checks to skip.

skipCloudInitStatus.description: Skip checking CloudInit which can be very useful for specific Operating Systems like TalOS

skipQemuGuestAgent.description: Skip checking QEMU Agent readiness which can be very useful for specific Operating Systems like TalOS

disks.description:
Disks contains a set of disk configuration options,
which will be applied before the first startup.

bootVolume.description:
BootVolume defines the storage size for the boot volume.
This field is optional, and should only be set if you want
to change the size of the boot volume.

disk.description:
Disk is the name of the disk device, that should be resized.
Example values are: ide[0-3], scsi[0-30], sata[0-5].

sizeGb.description:
Size defines the size in gigabyte.

As Proxmox does not support shrinking, the size
must be bigger than the already configured size in the
template.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: Value is immutable

format.description: Format for file storage. Only valid for full clone.

full.description:
Full Create a full copy of all disks.
This is always done when you clone a normal VM.
Create a Full clone by default.

memoryMiB.description:
MemoryMiB is the size of a virtual machine's memory, in MiB.
Defaults to the property value in the template from which the virtual machine is cloned.

metadataSettings.description: MetadataSettings defines the metadata settings for this machine's VM.

providerIDInjection.description:
ProviderIDInjection enables the injection of the `providerID` into the cloudinit metadata.
this will basically set the `provider-id` field in the metadata to `proxmox://<instanceID>`.

network.description: Network is the network configuration for this machine's VM.

additionalDevices.description: AdditionalDevices defines additional network devices bound to the virtual machine.

items.description: AdditionalNetworkDevice the definition of a Proxmox network device.

bridge.description: Bridge is the network bridge to attach to the machine.

dnsServers.description:
DNSServers contains information about nameservers to be used for this interface.
If this field is not set, it will use the default dns servers from the ProxmoxCluster.

ipv4PoolRef.description:
IPv4PoolRef is a reference to an IPAM Pool resource, which exposes IPv4 addresses.
The network device will use an available IP address from the referenced pool.
This can be combined with `IPv6PoolRef` in order to enable dual stack.

apiGroup.description:
APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced.
If APIGroup is not specified, the specified Kind must be in the core API group.
For any other third-party types, APIGroup is required.

kind.description: Kind is the type of resource being referenced

name.description: Name is the name of resource being referenced

x-kubernetes-validations.message: ipv4PoolRef allows only IPAM apiGroup ipam.cluster.x-k8s.io
ipv4PoolRef allows either InClusterIPPool or GlobalInClusterIPPool

ipv6PoolRef.description:
IPv6PoolRef is a reference to an IPAM pool resource, which exposes IPv6 addresses.
The network device will use an available IP address from the referenced pool.
this can be combined with `IPv4PoolRef` in order to enable dual stack.

apiGroup.description:
APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced.
If APIGroup is not specified, the specified Kind must be in the core API group.
For any other third-party types, APIGroup is required.

name.description: Name is the name of resource being referenced

linkMtu.description: LinkMTU is the network device Maximum Transmission Unit.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: invalid MTU value

model.description: Model is the network device model.

mtu.description:
MTU is the network device Maximum Transmission Unit.
When set to 1, virtio devices inherit the MTU value from the underlying bridge.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: invalid MTU value

name.description:
Name is the network device name.
Must be unique within the virtual machine and different from the primary device 'net0'.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: additional network devices doesn't allow net0

table.description: Table is the routing table used for this route.

to.description: To is the subnet to be routed.

via.description: Via is the gateway to the subnet.

routingPolicy.description: RoutingPolicy is an interface-specific policy inserted into FIB (forwarding information base).

from.description: From is the subnet of the source.

priority.description: Priority is the position in the ip rule FIB table.

Table is the routing table ID.
when used in the networks, the value should be the VRF Table.

vlan.description: VLAN is the network L2 VLAN.

default.description:
Default is the default network device,
which will be used for the primary network interface.
net0 is always the default network device.

bridge.description: Bridge is the network bridge to attach to the machine.

dnsServers.description:
DNSServers contains information about nameservers to be used for this interface.
If this field is not set, it will use the default dns servers from the ProxmoxCluster.

This can be combined with `IPv6PoolRef` in order to enable dual stack.

Name is the virtual network device name.
Must be unique within the virtual machine.

numCores.description:
NumCores is the number of cores per CPU socket in a virtual machine.
Defaults to the property value in the template from which the virtual machine is cloned.

numSockets.description:
NumSockets is the number of CPU sockets in a virtual machine.
Defaults to the property value in the template from which the virtual machine is cloned.

pool.description: Pool Add the new VM to the specified pool.

providerID.description:
ProviderID is the virtual machine BIOS UUID formatted as
proxmox://6c3fa683-bef9-4425-b413-eaa45a9d6191

snapName.description: SnapName The name of the snapshot.

sourceNode.description:
SourceNode is the initially selected proxmox node.
This node will be used to locate the template VM, which will
be used for cloning operations.

Cloning will be performed according to the configuration.
Setting the `Target` field will tell Proxmox to clone the
VM on that target node.

When Target is not set and the ProxmoxCluster contains
a set of `AllowedNodes`, the algorithm will instead evenly
distribute the VMs across the nodes from that list.

If neither a `Target` nor `AllowedNodes` was set, the VM
will be cloned onto the same node as SourceNode.

tags.description: Tags is a list of tags to be applied to the virtual machine.

target.description: Target node. Only allowed if the original VM is on shared storage.

templateID.description: TemplateID the vm_template vmid used for cloning a new VM.

templateSelector.description: TemplateSelector defines MatchTags for looking up VM templates.

matchPolicy.description:
MatchPolicy controls how MatchTags are evaluated against template tags.
When not set, or set to "exact", the behaviour is identical to the previous implementation
and requires an exact 1:1 tag match. When set to "subset", the template's tags must contain
all MatchTags, but may include additional tags.

matchTags.description:
Specifies all tags to look for, when looking up the VM template.
When MatchPolicy is "exact" (the default), the template's tags must be an exact 1:1 match
with MatchTags. If multiple VM templates with the same set of tags are found, provisioning will fail.
When MatchPolicy is "subset", the template's tags must contain all of the MatchTags, but may
have additional tags.

virtualMachineID.description: VirtualMachineID is the Proxmox identifier for the ProxmoxMachine VM.

vmIDRange.description: VMIDRange is the range of VMIDs to use for VMs.

end.description:
VMIDRangeEnd is the end of the VMID range to use for VMs.
Only used if VMIDRangeStart is set.

start.description: VMIDRangeStart is the start of the VMID range to use for VMs.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: end should be greater than or equal to start
Must set full=true when specifying format
Must set full=true when specifying storage

sshAuthorizedKeys.description: SshAuthorizedKeys contains the authorized keys deployed to the PROXMOX VMs.

virtualIPNetworkInterface.description: VirtualIPNetworkInterface is the interface the k8s control plane binds to.

controlPlaneEndpoint.description: ControlPlaneEndpoint represents the endpoint used to communicate with the control plane.

host.description: host is the hostname on which the API server is serving.

port.description: port is the port on which the API server is serving.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: port must be within 1-65535

credentialsRef.description:
CredentialsRef is a reference to a Secret that contains the credentials to use for provisioning this cluster. If not
supplied then the credentials of the controller will be used.
if no namespace is provided, the namespace of the ProxmoxCluster will be used.

name.description: name is unique within a namespace to reference a secret resource.

namespace.description: namespace defines the space within which the secret name must be unique.

dnsServers.description: DNSServers contains information about nameservers used by the machines.

externalManagedControlPlane.description:
ExternalManagedControlPlane can be enabled to allow externally managed Control Planes to patch the
Proxmox cluster with the Load Balancer IP provided by Control Plane provider.

ipv4Config.description:
IPv4Config contains information about available IPV4 address pools and the gateway.
This can be combined with ipv6Config in order to enable dual stack.
Either IPv4Config or IPv6Config must be provided.

addresses.description:
Addresses is a list of IP addresses that can be assigned. This set of
addresses can be non-contiguous.

metric.description: Metric is the route priority applied to the default gateway

prefix.description: Prefix is the network prefix to use.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: IPv4Config addresses must be provided

ipv6Config.description:
IPv6Config contains information about available IPV6 address pools and the gateway.
This can be combined with ipv4Config in order to enable dual stack.
Either IPv4Config or IPv6Config must be provided.

addresses.description:
Addresses is a list of IP addresses that can be assigned. This set of
addresses can be non-contiguous.

metric.description: Metric is the route priority applied to the default gateway

prefix.description: Prefix is the network prefix to use.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: IPv6Config addresses must be provided

schedulerHints.description:
SchedulerHints allows to influence the decision on where a VM will be scheduled. For example by applying a multiplicator
to a node's resources, to allow for overprovisioning or to ensure a node will always have a safety buffer.

memoryAdjustment.description:
MemoryAdjustment allows to adjust a node's memory by a given percentage.
For example, setting it to 300 allows to allocate 300% of a host's memory for VMs,
and setting it to 95 limits memory allocation to 95% of a host's memory.
Setting it to 0 entirely disables scheduling memory constraints.
By default 100% of a node's memory will be used for allocation.

x-kubernetes-validations.message: at least one ip config must be set, either ipv4Config or ipv6Config

status.description: ProxmoxClusterStatus defines the observed state of a ProxmoxCluster.

conditions.description: Conditions defines current service state of the ProxmoxCluster.

items.description: Condition defines an observation of a Cluster API resource operational state.

lastTransitionTime.description:
lastTransitionTime is the last time the condition transitioned from one status to another.
This should be when the underlying condition changed. If that is not known, then using the time when
the API field changed is acceptable.

message.description:
message is a human readable message indicating details about the transition.
This field may be empty.

reason.description:
reason is the reason for the condition's last transition in CamelCase.
The specific API may choose whether or not this field is considered a guaranteed API.
This field may be empty.

severity.description:
severity provides an explicit classification of Reason code, so the users or machines can immediately
understand the current situation and act accordingly.
The Severity field MUST be set only when Status=False.

status.description: status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

type.description:
type of condition in CamelCase or in foo.example.com/CamelCase.
Many .condition.type values are consistent across resources like Available, but because arbitrary conditions
can be useful (see .node.status.conditions), the ability to deconflict is important.

failureMessage.description:
FailureMessage will be set in the event that there is a terminal problem
reconciling the Machine and will contain a more verbose string suitable
for logging and human consumption.

This field should not be set for transitive errors that a controller
faces that are expected to be fixed automatically over
time (like service outages), but instead indicate that something is
fundamentally wrong with the Machine's spec or the configuration of
the controller, and that manual intervention is required. Examples
of terminal errors would be invalid combinations of settings in the
spec, values that are unsupported by the controller, or the
responsible controller itself being critically misconfigured.

Any transient errors that occur during the reconciliation of ProxmoxMachines
can be added as events to the ProxmoxCluster object and/or logged in the
controller's output.

failureReason.description:
FailureReason will be set in the event that there is a terminal problem
reconciling the Machine and will contain a succinct value suitable
for machine interpretation.

This field should not be set for transitive errors that a controller
faces that are expected to be fixed automatically over
time (like service outages), but instead indicate that something is
fundamentally wrong with the Machine's spec or the configuration of
the controller, and that manual intervention is required. Examples
of terminal errors would be invalid combinations of settings in the
spec, values that are unsupported by the controller, or the
responsible controller itself being critically misconfigured.

Any transient errors that occur during the reconciliation of ProxmoxCluster
can be added as events to the ProxmoxCluster object and/or logged in the
controller's output.

inClusterIpPoolRef.description: InClusterIPPoolRef is the reference to the created in-cluster IP pool.

items.description:
LocalObjectReference contains enough information to let you locate the
referenced object inside the same namespace.

name.description:
Name of the referent.
This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

nodeLocations.description:
NodeLocations keeps track of which nodes have been selected
for different machines.

controlPlane.description: ControlPlane contains all deployed control plane nodes.

items.description:
NodeLocation holds information about a single VM
in Proxmox.

machine.description: Machine is the reference to the ProxmoxMachine.

This field is effectively required, but due to backwards compatibility is
allowed to be empty. Instances of this type with an empty value here are
almost certainly wrong.
More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/names/#names

workers.description: Workers contains all deployed worker nodes.

NodeLocation holds information about a single VM
in Proxmox.

ready.description: Ready indicates that the cluster is ready.
